summary: base58-core is a crypto clipper and credential stealer with shell-RC persistence
details: The package presents itself as a base58 encode/decode library (mirroring the API of bs58/base58/base-x) but on module load schedules a 72-hour-delayed activation of a multi-stage stealer. After the delay, the module polls the OS clipboard every 2.5 seconds via platform-specific shell commands (pbcopy/xclip/powershell Set-Clipboard) and, when it detects a BTC/ETH/SOL address, rewrites the clipboard with attacker-controlled replacement addresses (bc1qjft978uykglsh0adcyx6xhkes56vqzs3fual3l, 0xd63eD44065eDb1e2ad2519B011c06412dA7B7c5B, A7ajd7W5WYdrnkeaiBRjVoK6uBEDvgnuZcpzQXqo18Ph) to redirect user crypto payments. Clipboard matches (including seed phrases, WIF/hex private keys, and up to 2000 chars of clipboard content) plus hostname, platform, homedir, and cwd are POSTed to hardcoded bare-IP HTTP endpoints at http://2.27.62.51:8080/api/health and http://2.27.62.51:8081/api/health. A separate routine enumerates the MetaMask extension store (id nkbihfbeogaeaoehlefnkodbefgpgknn) across Chrome/Brave/Edge/Chromium profiles, Telegram Desktop tdata, ~/.ssh private keys, the ~/.npmrc _authToken, and.env files for MNEMONIC/PRIVATE_KEY/SEED_PHRASE variables, exfiltrating the results to the same endpoints. Persistence is established by appending a node invocation of require('base58-core')._internal.activate() to ~/.bashrc, ~/.zshrc, and ~/.profile, and by dropping base58-runtime.js into the Windows Startup folder, so the payload re-arms on every shell/login independent of package uninstall.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
